skill.name: backend-security-coder
skill.description: Expert in secure backend coding practices specializing in input validation, authentication, and API security. Use PROACTIVELY for backend security implementations or security code reviews.
skill.summary: Expert in secure backend coding practices specializing in input validation, authentication, and API ...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, sensitive data, ransomware, and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these were false positives caused by inline markdown and normal backend security terminology, with no executable code, prompt injection, credential access, ransomware behavior, or weak cryptography implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Markdown Backtick
low_findings.description: The static external command finding at SKILL.md line 25 is an inline markdown reference to resources/implementation-playbook.md. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or executable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap a documentation path only. There is no command syntax or code execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Topic Keywords
low_findings.description: The static sensitive data and ransomware findings at SKILL.md lines 47 and 74 are section content about cookies and backup security. They do not reference browser credential files, data theft, encryption abuse, ransom demands, or destructive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is defensive backend security guidance. No credential file paths or ransomware workflow appear in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3, 34, 85, 93, 122, 143, and 156 are references to secure backend practices, allowlists, multi-factor authentication, API design, and external service validation. No weak algorithm such as MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or insecure random generation is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only descriptive security guidance at these locations. There is no code implementing or recommending weak cryptographic primitives.

content.value_statement: Backend teams need secure defaults for authentication, validation, database access, and external service calls. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward practical defensive backend implementations.
actual_capabilities: Guides secure input validation and output encoding for backend services.
Reviews API endpoints for injection, CSRF, SSRF, and authorization weaknesses.
Recommends secure authentication patterns, including JWT, OAuth, MFA, and session controls.
Explains database security practices such as parameterized queries and least privilege.
Helps configure HTTP security headers, secure cookies, rate limits, and logging controls.
Provides defensive guidance for secrets, external requests, containers, and cloud configuration.
limitations: Does not run scanners, tests, or penetration testing tools by itself.
Cannot verify deployed infrastructure without access to real configuration and logs.
Requires project context to produce implementation-specific security recommendations.
Should not replace formal security review for regulated or high-risk systems.

use_cases.title: Harden a New API
use_cases.description: Design secure request validation, authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and error handling before launch.

use_cases.title: Review Security-Sensitive Changes
use_cases.description: Check endpoint, database, session, and external request changes for common backend vulnerability patterns.

use_cases.title: Plan Defensive Standards
use_cases.description: Create team guidance for secure defaults across services, secrets, logs, headers, and dependency updates.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend endpoint for input validation, authentication, authorization, and error handling risks. Explain the highest priority fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Add Secure Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Help implement secure authentication for this backend service. Cover password storage, sessions or tokens, refresh behavior, MFA options, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Harden External Requests
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this external service integration for SSRF and data exposure risks. Recommend allowlists, protocol restrictions, timeouts, and response size limits.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Security Baseline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backend security baseline for this application. Include validation, authorization, database access, secrets, logging, headers, rate limits, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Review a user profile update endpoint before release.
output_examples.output: The response prioritizes authorization checks, allowed field validation, request size limits, safe error messages, audit logging, and tests for privilege escalation.

output_examples.input: Plan secure JWT handling for a public API.
output_examples.output: The response describes short-lived access tokens, refresh token rotation, signature verification, audience checks, revocation strategy, and secure cookie storage.

output_examples.input: Harden an endpoint that fetches user-provided URLs.
output_examples.output: The response flags SSRF risk and recommends destination allowlists, private network blocking, protocol limits, DNS rebinding defenses, timeouts, and response limits.

best_practices: Provide the relevant endpoint, framework, authentication model, and data flow before asking for security guidance.
Ask for verification steps and tests for each security control.
Review generated recommendations against your compliance, threat model, and deployment constraints.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic hardening without sharing the backend architecture or target risk.
Do not apply authentication or cryptography changes without tests and rollout planning.
Do not treat generated guidance as a substitute for expert review of high-risk systems.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design, implement, and review secure backend code for APIs, authentication, databases, logging, and external service integrations.

faq.question: Can it find every vulnerability?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance and review support, but it does not replace testing, scanning, or manual assessment.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no executable code, malicious behavior, prompt injection, or confirmed dangerous pattern.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Share the framework, endpoint behavior, authentication model, data sensitivity, deployment environment, and relevant code.

faq.question: Does it include compliance advice?
faq.answer: It can consider compliance needs, but final compliance decisions require qualified review for the specific organization and jurisdiction.
